warnings: The sum of Fukui functions f+ and f- both equal 1.0000, with a note that a large drift suggests an N+1 or N−1 state may not have fully converged, indicating some caution in interpreting the absolute values.

prose: The Fukui reactivity analysis on benzaldehyde was successfully computed using DFT at the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in the gas phase. The analysis provides various reactive indices and visualizations, including the most electrophilic and nucleophilic sites. The method used was B3LYP/def2-TZVP at the DFT program level. Warnings: The sum of Fukui functions f+ and f- both equal 1.0000, with a note that a large drift suggests an N+1 or N−1 state may not have fully converged, indicating some caution in interpreting the absolute values.
